preamble: The shift from the voiced alveolar fricative [z] to a retroflex stop (like [ɖ]) is a form of fortition and backing. It occurs when a sibilant sound retracts its place of articulation and strengthens into a full stop, often under the influence of neighboring retroflex sounds.

examples.note: Stages in the development of retroflex series where alveolar sounds retracted and hardened.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
